content:
# Joshua Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 226–227 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_002_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_002_page_002.jpg`

1. And Yehoshua son of Nun sent from the Shittim two men, spies, secretly, saying, "Go, see the land, and Yericho." And they went and came [to] the house of a woman, a harlot, and her name [was] Rachav, and they lay down there.

2. And it was told to the king of Yericho, saying, "Behold, men have come here tonight from the sons of Yisrael to search out the land."

3. And the king of Yericho sent to Rachav, saying, "Bring out the men who have come to you, who have come to your house, for they have come to search out all the land."

4. And the woman took the two men and hid him, and she said, "Yes, the men came to me, but I did not know from where they [were].

5. And it was [at] the shutting of the gate in the darkness, and the men went out. I do not know where the men went. Pursue quickly after them, for you will overtake them."

6. But she had brought them up to the roof, and she hid them among the stalks of flax which were arranged for her upon the roof.

7. And the men pursued after them [on] the way of the Yarden, unto the fords, and the gate they shut after those who were pursuing went out after them.

8. And before they lay down, she went up to them upon the roof.

9. And she said to the men, "I know that YHWH has given to you the land, and that the terror of you has fallen upon us, and that all the inhabitants of the land have melted before you.

10. For we have heard how YHWH dried up the waters of the Sea of Reeds before you when you came out from Mitsrayim, and what you did to the two kings of the Amorite who [were] beyond the Yarden — to Sichon and to Og — whom you utterly destroyed.

11. And we heard, and our heart melted, and there did not rise up any more spirit in any man because of you, for YHWH your God — he [is] God in the heavens above and upon the earth beneath.

12. And now, swear, please, to me by YHWH, since I have dealt with you in loyal-love, that you also will deal with the house of my father in loyal-love. And give to me a sign of truth,

13. and you shall keep alive my father and my mother and my brothers and my sisters and all who [belong] to them, and you shall deliver our souls from death."

14. And the men said to her, "Our soul [shall die] instead of yours, if you do not tell this matter of ours. And it shall be, when YHWH gives to us the land, that we will deal with you in loyal-love and in truth."

15. And she let them down by a rope through the window, for her house [was] in the wall of the fortification, and in the fortification she was dwelling.

16. And she said to them, "To the hill-country go, lest the pursuers encounter you, and hide yourselves there three days until the pursuers return, and afterward you shall go on your way."

17. And the men said to her, "We [will be] free from this oath of yours which you have made us swear.

18. Behold, when we come into the land, this cord of scarlet thread you shall bind in the window through which you let us down, and your father and your mother and your brothers and all the house of your father you shall gather to you, into the house.

19. And it shall be, everyone who goes out from the doors of your house into the outside, his blood [shall be] upon his head, and we [shall be] free. And everyone who is with you in the house, his blood [shall be] upon our head if a hand is upon him.

20. And if you tell this matter of ours, then we shall be free from your oath which you have made us swear."

21. And she said, "According to your words, so [be] it." And she sent them, and they went. And she bound the scarlet cord in the window.

22. And they went and came to the hill-country, and they remained there three days until the pursuers returned. And the pursuers searched all the way and did not find [them].

23. And the two men returned and came down from the hill-country, and they crossed over and came to Yehoshua son of Nun, and they recounted to him all the things that had found them.

24. And they said to Yehoshua, "Surely YHWH has given into our hand all the land, and also all the inhabitants of the land have melted before us."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

**Verse 1 — "the Shittim" (הַשִּׁטִּים):** The definite article is present in the Leningrad Codex; the full toponym is "the Shittim" (lit. "the acacias"), referring to Abel-Shittim in the plains of Moav (cf. Num 25:1; 33:49).

**Verse 1 — "Rachav" (רָחָב):** Transliterated from the Hebrew. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads Ρααβ (Raab). The name likely derives from the root r-ch-b, "wide/broad."

**Verse 4 — "hid him" (וַתִּצְפְּנוֹ):** The Masoretic Text has the singular pronominal suffix ("him"), though two men are in view. This is preserved literally. Some manuscripts and the LXX reflect a plural. The singular may be a collective or distributive usage.

**Verse 5 — "in the darkness" (בַּחֹשֶׁךְ):** The Leningrad Codex reads בַּחֹשֶׁךְ. Some translations render "at dark" or "when it was dark." The literal rendering is retained.

**Verse 6 — "stalks of flax" (פִּשְׁתֵּי הָעֵץ):** Literally "flaxes of the tree/wood," i.e., stalks of flax laid out for drying on the roof — a common practice in the harvest season.

**Verse 10 — "utterly destroyed" (הֶחֱרַמְתֶּם):** From the root ch-r-m, referring to the devotion-ban (cherem). The hiphil form indicates "you devoted [them] to destruction."

**Verse 14 — "Our soul instead of yours" (נַפְשֵׁנוּ תַחְתֵּיכֶם לָמוּת):** Literally "Our soul instead-of-you to die." The men pledge their own lives as surety. The construction is compressed in Hebrew — the bracketed [shall die] is implied from the infinitive לָמוּת.

**Verse 15 — "fortification" (חוֹמָה):** The word חוֹמָה denotes a defensive wall or fortification. Rachav's house was built into the city wall structure itself, a detail confirmed by archaeological parallels at Yericho.

**Verse 18 — "cord of scarlet thread" (תִּקְוַת חוּט הַשָּׁנִי):** The word תִּקְוָה can mean "cord/rope" or "hope" — a richly layered term. Here it refers to the physical scarlet cord, though the theological resonance with "hope" (same root) is noted. The LXX (Vaticanus) renders τὸ σπαρτίον τὸ κόκκινον ("the scarlet rope").

**Verse 23 — "all the things that had found them" (כָּל־הַמֹּצְאוֹת אֹתָם):** Literally "all the findings/things-found [to] them" — an unusual construction. The participle הַמֹּצְאוֹת functions as a substantive: "the things that had befallen/found them." This awkward phrasing is preserved per EMET methodology.

**Verse 24 — "Surely" (כִּי):** The כִּי here functions as an asseverative particle, expressing certainty: "Surely" or "Indeed." The spies' report echoes Rachav's own confession (v. 9).

**LXX Comparison (Vaticanus):** The Vaticanus manuscript (Vat.gr.1209, folio 164) preserves Joshua 2 with minor expansions. Notable divergences include the plural pronoun in v. 4 (αὐτούς vs. MT singular suffix), and in v. 15 the LXX specifies κατεχάλασεν αὐτοὺς διὰ τῆς θυρίδος ("she let them down through the window"), closely matching MT. The chapter division and verse structure align between traditions.

— *EMET*
